name: Block 17
description: A monolithic block of concrete apartments. Its windows leer, balconies jutting at impossible angles. Someone or something waits at every threshold.

characters.name: Abram Simonis
characters.backstory: Self appointed superintendant of the apartment complex. He marks arrivals, but claims to have never seen one. Only departures and absences.

encounters.question: Do you remember? When the corridors breathed with more than just dust mites and my counting?

responses.text: Each grain of settled silence... proves you belong here and counted. Catalogued, crystallized in the amber of my vigilance... Your footsteps align with the creaking I've memorized since the exodus.
Memory's a luxury for those who haven't watched paint peel in real-time... Keep your nostalgia... I traffic in present tense infractions and the weight of 'keys' that open nothing...

encounters.question: Have you noticed? How your shadow, falls different from the stains that preceded you?

responses.text: Different is disorder... Your edges blur, where theirs held firm and a smudge on my ledger. An asterisk in the margin. I'll file you under 'pending' review until you learn to cast darkness correctly...
Every shadow here earns its shape through repetition. Yours flickers like a moth against protocol, throwing patterns I've spent decades straightening. Consistency is the only currency left.

encounters.question: Will you clean the... handprints... from my door that weren't there yesterday?

responses.text: Those marks... Predate your arrival by years... And you're just now seeing what was always there! I preserve evidence... Not erase it... Each smudge is a census of the gone.
Your door collects what you attract... I... maintain order. Not innocence... Wipe your own ghosts OR wear them. MY jurisdiction ends at 'structural' integrity.

characters.name: Lost Resident
characters.backstory: Claims to have lived here for years, but can't remember any door leading out.

encounters.question: Did we share a mailbox once, or am I conflating you with the taste of rust?

responses.text: Rust and recognition bleed the same copper through my teeth. Maybe we did, maybe we will and time's a lazy river here, doubling back through the same silt. Your face fits the empty frame.
Mailboxes are for people with addresses. I'm more like the echo between apartment numbers, the draft that forgets which window birthed it. Don't pin me to your timeline.

encounters.question: Is this the third floor or have I been climbing the same flight since Tuesday?

responses.text: Tuesday was vertical too and I remember because the stairwell hiccupped and Wednesday never came. We're wherever legs stop mattering and arrival becomes academic. Third floor, thirty-third, does it matter when the door numbers swim?
Floors are a conspiracy of concrete and consensus. Count if it comforts you, but I've been here long enough to know ascending and descending taste identical in the dark.

encounters.question: Can you help me find my unit—the one with the morning light?

responses.text: Morning light lives in 4B, 7G, and the space between my ribs where I store tomorrow's disappointments. I'll walk you there if you promise to remember me when you find it and I'm collecting witnesses to prove I existed.
Your unit's wherever you stop looking. Morning's just another rumor the windows spread to keep us searching. I gave up on coordinates when the hallways started breathing backwards.
